title: KR Atom Record
description: Schema for a single atom record — the output of the atomization engine (محرك التذرير) consumed by the excerpting engine (محرك الاقتطاف). An atom is the smallest indivisible unit of text within a passage, typed and precisely located by character offsets. Source-agnostic: operates below the normalization boundary.

atom_id.description: Globally unique identifier: {book_id}:{layer}:{six_digit_sequence}. Sequence numbers are assigned in reading order within each layer, globally sequential within a source — never reset between passages. Immutable once assigned.

passage_id.description: ID of the passage this atom belongs to. Links atom to its parent passage for pipeline traceability.

book_id.description: Canonical source identifier. Must be consistent across all records from the same source. Added for pipeline traceability.

science_id.description: Science classification inherited from the passage. Carries science identity through the pipeline per §7.3. Added for pipeline traceability.

atom_type.description: Structural type determining behavior in excerpting. heading: section/chapter heading (excluded from excerpts, used in heading_path). prose_sentence: single complete Arabic sentence (most common). bonded_cluster: semantically inseparable multi-sentence unit (requires bonded_cluster_trigger). verse_evidence: poetry cited as evidence. quran_quote_standalone: standalone Quran text. list_item: numbered/bulleted item in an enumeration.

source_layer.description: Source layer. matn: main text. footnote: editor footnotes. tahqiq_3ilmi: substantive editorial commentary. sharh/hashiya: classical multi-layer commentary.

atom_text.description: Verbatim text of this atom, copied exactly from the source passage. Never edited, rewritten, or normalized after atomization. Footnote markers are stripped but recorded in footnote_refs. This is the ground truth for all downstream processing.

anchor_span.description: Character-level position of this atom within its passage's text (passage_text field). The invariant atom_text == passage_text[start:end] must hold.

start.description: Start character offset (inclusive) in Unicode codepoints.

end.description: End character offset (exclusive) in Unicode codepoints.

page_hint.description: Approximate page reference in the printed edition (e.g., 'ص:٢١'). Informational only — helps human reviewers locate atoms in physical sources.

context_role.description: When this atom serves as context in an excerpt, its functional role (e.g., 'evidence', 'classification_frame', 'prerequisite'). Null when not yet assigned a context role.

bonded_cluster_trigger.description: REQUIRED non-null when atom_type='bonded_cluster'. Explains why multiple sentences were merged. Null otherwise.

trigger_id.description: Identifier for the bonding trigger type.

reason.description: Explanation of why the sentences are bonded.

atomization_notes.description: Free-text for non-trigger decisions (heading recognition, verse identification, page-span notes). Null for straightforward atoms.

items.description: An embedded content marker or anomaly flag within the atom's text.

internal_tags.description: List of embedded content markers within this atom's text. Used to identify Quran/hadith/verse fragments embedded in prose and to document source inconsistencies.

items.description: A footnote reference linking this atom to a footnote.

footnote_atom_id.description: atom_id of the corresponding footnote-layer atom, if resolved.

footnote_refs.description: Matn-to-footnote linkage. Empty for footnote-layer atoms.

relation.description: Optional typed relation to another atom (e.g., answer relationship for exercises). Null for atoms with no relations.
